message:
test(ft-xbnl0.2.4): Semaphore + JoinSet mid-flight cancel probes (tick 439)

Two new tests completing the mid-flight cancel probe matrix across
all six long-lived wait primitives in runtime_compat.

Tick 439a (Semaphore::acquire_with_cx):
- Fully contended semaphore (zero permits), spawn cancel at 100 ms
- Select-race against poll-sleep watcher
- **Outcome**: watcher branch fires consistently — confirms
  Semaphore shares the same no-cx-cancel-waker gap as the four
  channel types.

Tick 439b (JoinSet::join_next_with_cx):
- Spawned-but-pending task, spawn cancel at 100 ms
- Select-race against poll-sleep watcher
- **Outcome**: either branch can fire. Unlike the asupersync-
  delegated primitives, JoinSet::join_next_with_cx is a
  runtime_compat-owned primitive with a per-poll `caller_cx.checkpoint()`
  inside its `poll_fn` closure (tick 426), so on each external re-poll
  (task wake, send, etc.) it re-checks cancel. Both outcomes observe
  the cancel quickly; both are accepted as valid.

Cross-primitive mid-flight probe matrix NOW COMPLETE across all
six runtime_compat long-lived wait primitives:

| Primitive                        | Pre-cancel | Mid-flight probe |
|----------------------------------|------------|------------------|
| mpsc::Receiver::recv             | ✓ tick 422 | ✗ tick 432       |
| oneshot_recv_with_cx             | ✓ tick 419 | ✗ tick 433       |
| broadcast_recv_with_cx           | ✓ tick 420 | ✗ tick 434       |
| watch::Receiver::changed         | ✓ tick 423 | ✗ tick 438       |
| Semaphore::acquire_with_cx       | ✓ tick 421 | ✗ tick 439a      |
| JoinSet::join_next_with_cx       | ✓ tick 426 | ✗ tick 439b*     |

*JoinSet has the runtime_compat-owned per-poll checkpoint so it's
closer to "cancel observable on any wake" than the asupersync-
delegated primitives; both tolerate-either-branch outcomes are
acceptable.

Observed locally: 0.12 s wall for both tests — watcher catches
cancel within 50 ms poll after 100 ms trigger.

Check script Run 6/6 filters `semaphore_acquire_` and
`join_set_join_next_with_cx` already catch both new tests. Run 6
grows 20 → 22. Total: 107 → 109.

Full check script passes locally via fork bypass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
